title: No relief for Wangchuk: HC backs govt's move to shift Sonam to hospital, says no force used
content: The Delhi HC upheld the Centre's decision to move Sonam Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital.  The court found the government's action was not arbitrary given his health condition. His wife's petition for transfer to a private hospital was declined by the court.  The court noted Wangchuk's family has been given unrestricted access to him.

title: Relief for Abhishek Banerjee: HC halts demolition drive of TMC MP's Amtala office
content: The Calcutta High Court has ordered a halt to demolition work at Abhishek Banerjee's office. This directive came after an anti-encroachment drive was initiated at the premises. The Trinamool Congress leader had alleged political motivation behind the demolition efforts. He stated the office was built legally on purchased land with permissions. The district administration claimed the structure violated building plan rules.

title: 'Sorry papa': Kanpur BTech gold medallist dies by suicide after 50 failed job attempts
content: Tragedy struck in Kanpur as a 23-year-old BTech gold medallist ended his life, having faced the daunting challenge of applying for a government position more than fifty times without success. His poignant suicide note expressed sorrow towards his father for not meeting expectations. The police have launched an investigation and are exploring the likelihood that depression contributed to this sorrowful decision.

title: Musk's population alarm reaches China as govt tells tech firms: Stop making AI chatbots
content: China introduced new rules to stop AI chatbots encouraging emotional dependence.  These regulations aim to address the nation's declining birth rate and shrinking population.  Companies must now obtain government review before releasing companion AI services.  Virtual romantic relationships with minors are also strictly prohibited under these rules.  Authorities can shut down services deemed unsafe, promoting real-world connections.

title: 'NCPI sought recognition, why object?' Rijiju on opposition walkout in TMC's support
content: Union minister Kiren Rijiju defended inviting the Nationalist Citizens Party of India to an all-party meeting. Opposition parties staged a symbolic walkout protesting the invitation to rebel Trinamool Congress MPs. Rijiju stated the government followed parliamentary norms and could not ignore the party's recognition request. Eight bills are listed for the upcoming Monsoon Session, which begins Monday. All parties have the right to express views and cooperate for the nation.

title: Rohit, Virat create history at Lord's, become first Indian pair to...
content: Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli reached 400 international matches together. This milestone occurred during the third ODI against England at Lord's. Both players aim to win the 2027 Cricket World Cup. England won the toss and chose to bat first in the series decider. Jasprit Bumrah was ruled out of the match due to injury.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: 5-year-old boy in Batman costume saves a drowning man
content: Dressed as Batman, a young boy noticed a man stranded on a sandbank and sprang into action. He quickly informed his family, who then reached out to the coastguard and RNLI for help. A lifeboat arrived rapidly, ensuring the safe rescue of the man and his two dogs from the rising tide. This quick thinking by the boy undoubtedly played a crucial role in saving someone's life.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Why are players wearing black armbands during the India vs England Lord's ODI?
content: Players from India and England wore black armbands to honor Sir Gary Sobers.  This tribute occurred before the third and final ODI match at Lord's.  England captain Harry Brook won the toss and elected to bat first.  India made several changes to their team due to player injuries.  The match was significant with the ODI World Cup approaching.

title: Planning a large building? Centre proposes mandatory energy star ratings before completion
content: New buildings will soon require energy performance ratings.  The government proposes a mandatory star rating system for large structures.  Owners must register and submit compliance documents to the Bureau of Energy Efficiency.  A provisional certificate will be issued after initial review.  Final ratings will be provided within thirty days of submission.

title: Ex-techie suffering from mental health issues walks naked to temple, dies by suicide
content: A 25-year-old former techie died by suicide after experiencing mental health issues.  She walked naked to a temple and then jumped into a pond.  The woman and her mother were reportedly suffering from phobias and fears.  Police recovered her body from the pond on Saturday morning.  Her father, who lives separately, was informed of the incident.

title: Maple Leafs' Gavin McKenna trains with Sharks star Macklin Celebrini
content: Toronto Maple Leafs’ prospect Gavin McKenna was recently spotted in a competitive offseason skate in Vancouver with some of hockey's brightest young talent. The training session gave fans a glimpse of the next generation of the NHL with San Jose Sharks forward Macklin Celebrini, Chicago Blackhawks prospect Michael Misa and San Jose Sharks defenseman Sam Dickinson.

title: How much prize money does the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place winner get? Full payout explained
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will award twenty-nine million dollars for third place. The fourth-place team will receive twenty-seven million dollars after the playoff. This prize money is separate from preparation payments given to nations. The champions will earn fifty million dollars and runners-up thirty-three million dollars. FIFA confirmed prize money distribution for all tournament stages.

title: Former NHL star Mike Commodore warns Oilers parents at development camp
content: Ex-NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has escalated his years-long war on the Edmonton Oilers by issuing a warning to parents attending the club’s development camp. The social media post went viral across the NHL, reigniting discussion of Commodore’s past with former coach Mike Babcock, and drawing mixed reactions from hockey fans and analysts.

title: 'Tear gas guns arrived': CJP says BJP 'goons', Delhi Police lurking to 'create mischief'
content: In another post, the CJP posted a photo of a security personnel sitting with a tear gas gun at the protest site. Earlier, Wangchuk described the proposed July 20 Parliament march as India's "second freedom movement", calling for "freedom from fear" and "freedom from injustice" while urging supporters to make the mobilisation a "big success".

title: Sleepless nights, depressing times, and then a Grandmaster: Making of Rathanvel VS
content: After four-and-a-half years of heartbreak, sleepless nights and repeated near-misses, Rathanvel VS finally crossed the 2500 Elo mark to become India's 99th Grandmaster. From discovering chess through a free board to overcoming injuries, financial setbacks and crushing disappointments, the 2001-born Coimbatore player's perseverance, family support and belief transformed a long-awaited dream into reality.

title: 'I don't want to go to jail': Spain star's Donald Trump joke before World Cup final
content: Spanish forward Borja Iglesias humorously anticipates meeting US President Donald Trump at the World Cup final. He hopes the handshake will be brief and uneventful after the match. Spain has reached the final after an impressive defensive performance throughout the tournament. Argentina aims to defend their 2022 World Cup title in this championship match. This final marks the first time European and Copa America champions will compete.

title: Govt delays another key report as it seeks opposition support on delimitation bill
content: Two parliamentary committees have postponed approving their reports on significant legislation. This development occurs as the government seeks opposition support for other political proposals. The anti-graft bill and higher education regulator bills face widespread protest. Both bills may not be presented during the current parliamentary session. Congress criticizes the government's handling of these controversial legislative matters.

title: Delhi among world's cheapest cities for property despite lowest salaries
content: Delhi offers affordable property and daily living, according to a new report.  However, residents earn some of the world's lowest salaries after taxes.  Property prices in Delhi remain relatively low compared to other global cities.  Daily expenses like coffee are among the cheapest worldwide for consumers.  Despite affordability, Delhi did not rank high in quality of life metrics.

title: Messi aims to accomplish in New York what Maradona couldn't 36 years ago
content: It is also the first time since Diego Maradona inspired Argentina to back-to-back World Cup finals in 1986 and 1990 that the country has reached consecutive title matches. Argentina won the World Cup in 1986 before finishing runners-up in 1990. Their seven final appearances have come in 1930, 1978, 1986, 1990, 2014, 2022 and 2026.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 10 [LIVE]: Ajay Devgn's comedy headed Rs 120 cr India net milestone
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Dhamaal 4' continues its impressive second weekend run at the Indian box office. After comfortably crossing the Rs 100 crore India net mark on Day 8 and registering a strong Saturday jump on Day 9, the comedy entertainer has now entered Day 10 with Rs 112.46 crore India net (live). The Indra Kumar directorial is now targeting a Rs 115 crore India net finish by the end of its second weekend, while the worldwide gross is expected to move closer to the Rs 160 crore milestone, despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'.

title: 'Forced to work 15 hours a day, no salary for 4 months': Hyderabad woman stranded in Oman
content: A Hyderabad woman alleges forced labor and wage denial in Oman. She traveled for a housemaid job but faced poor conditions and no pay. The woman escaped and sought help from the Indian Embassy in Muscat. The embassy has acknowledged the matter and is investigating the situation. Officials are working to ensure her safety and arrange her return home.

title: RRB ALP CBT 2 city slip 2026 released for July 28 exam, admit card expected soon
content: Railway Recruitment Board has released the RRB ALP CBT 2 City Intimation Slip 2026 for candidates who qualified the Stage 1 examination under ALP CEN 01/2025. The city slip was issued on July 18, 2026, for the CBT 2 exam scheduled on July 28, 2026. A total of 1,58,705 candidates can download the document using their application number and date of birth. The admit cards are expected before the examination.

title: Punjab cop, who targeted NRIs in US, held in Rs 3.8-cr extortion bid linked to AAP worker's murder
content: Punjab Police arrested inspector Gurinderjit Singh Nagra, former Tanda SHO.  He faces charges of extortion and corruption linked to a US family.  The US justice department seeks his extradition for conspiracy to commit extortion.  Nagra allegedly extorted Rs 16 lakh from the US-based family.  This case is connected to the murder of an AAP worker.

title: 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' writer reveals real story behind partition drama
content: Screenwriter Nayanika Mahtani shares her personal connection to the film 'Main Vaapas Aaunga'.  Her grandparents were refugees during India's Partition, inspiring her writing.  Director Imtiaz Ali developed the film's core idea from a real-life encounter.  The story centers on an elderly man's unfinished business in his ancestral village.  A 'Chaand Baali' element also originated from a real incident with a friend.

title: China building world's largest naval replenishment ship to boost carrier operations
content: China is developing a new and larger naval replenishment vessel at Guangzhou Shipyard. This advanced vessel is expected to exceed the size of current Type 901 support ships, bolstering the People's Liberation Army Navy's ability to operate effectively at long ranges. Its impressive dimensions may even rival those of the US Navy's replenishment fleet, reflecting China's ambitions for extended carrier operations beyond its territorial waters.

title: Before kick-off, Argentina vs Spain final has already entered World Cup history
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 final features Argentina and Spain, the top two ranked teams.  Argentina seeks consecutive titles, a feat not achieved since Brazil in 1962.  Spain aims for their second World Cup, boasting an impressive thirty-seven-game unbeaten streak.  This match marks Lionel Messi's first encounter with Spain since 2010.  Spain's defense has been exceptional, conceding only one goal throughout the tournament.

title: 'Your silence is unacceptable': Rahul, Kharge write to PM Modi on Ram Mandir donation theft
content: Demanding a probe into financial affairs of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, both the leaders said that the "findings and the Trust's accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been utilised". ​Ahead of the Monoon Session of the Parliament, the leader of the opposition in the Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, and the Rajya Sabha, Mallikarjun Kharge, urged PM to "immediately order an independent and comprehensive investigation.

title: PV Sindhu scripts history, stuns Yamaguchi to become first Indian to ...
content: The 31-year-old became the first Indian to win the Japan Open after beating the three-time world champion 21-17, 21-17. The victory ended her title drought of more than two years and marked her biggest title since winning the World Championships in 2019.

title: 'Ramayana': Ranbir Kapoor touches Sunny Deol and Kumar Vishwas' feet at the Pratham Sankalp event
content: At 'Ramayana's Pratham Sankalp event, Ranbir Kapoor touched Sunny Deol and Kumar Vishwas's feet, winning hearts online. Ranbir, Sai Pallavi and Sunny shared heartfelt moments with media. Starring Ranbir, Sai Pallavi, Yash and Sunny, the Rs 4,000 crore epic releases in two parts—Diwali 2026 and Diwali 2027.

title: Call records, chats, deleted files: Chilling details in Lucknow scientist's death
content: Police are examining Rahul Singh's smartphone for digital evidence related to his death.  An FIR was registered against four individuals for abetment to suicide charges.  Rahul's brother alleged sustained harassment and financial disputes led to his extreme step.  Investigators are reviewing communications and financial transactions for crucial details.  The forensic report will determine the exact cause of the scientist's death.

title: Call for action against private mini bus operators for collecting excess fares from the public
content: S. Manogaran, President of the Association said that the RTO had been briefed about the collection of excess fares by operators of private mini buses in the district

title: KUHS, KVASU sign MoU to strengthen academic and research collaboration
content: The MoU was exchanged at a function held at the Verghese Kurien Institute of Dairy Technology on the Mannuthy Veterinary College campus

title: Naveen Patnaik asks Centre to initiate dialogue with students
content: A nation that compromises the integrity of its examination system, compromises its own future and this grave issue, affecting millions of students across the country, must be taken up in Parliament as the highest priority, Naveen Patnaik says

title: NEET-UG: NTA warns against fake, AI-generated OMR sheets; legal action may follow
content: The warning comes after the NTA reviewed several complaints regarding differences between candidates' expected and declared NEET 2026 scores

title: Oommen Chandy set the benchmark for public service, says Kerala Minister Anil Kumar
content: He says the late leader always stood by the most vulnerable sections of society and did everything within his capacity to help improve their lives

title: CJP protest in Bengaluru urges more participation, sustained questioning
content: While the protest centred around the NEET paper leak and what the movement termed a “deteriorating democratic system”, it went beyond that agenda on July 19

title: Delhi HC declines interim relief on plea by Wangchuk’s wife seeking transfer from Safdarjung Hospital
content: The Court accepted the Centre’s stand that shifting him to Safdarjung was a medical decision, not an arbitrary one

title: Rule changes on the cards to streamline land conversion, title deed distribution, says Kerala Revenue Minister
content: Anil Kumar says government will examine practical suggestions and proposals for legal amendments, and take appropriate decisions, once review meetings in all 14 districts are completed

title: Pinarayi Vijayan expresses solidarity with protesters at Jantar Mantar
content: Kerala Leader of the Opposition says the ongoing protests are in defence of democratic rights and the voices of the protesters will not surrender to ‘authoritarian rule of Sangh Parivar and Narendra Modi government’

title: Srisailam temple management makes changes in darshan arrangements to improve darshan for common devotees
content: On Saturday, Sunday and Monday Sparsha Darshan’ during morning hours would be provided exclusively to devotees who have obtained tickets in advance via the 'Manamitra’ WhatsApp service or online platforms

title: Madhya Pradesh Cabinet approves UCC draft bill for presentation in State Assembly
content: The bill seeks to completely prohibit polygamy and multiple marriages across all communities, while also declaring practices like nikaha halala as illegal and punishable offences

title: Jammu and Kashmir rains LIVE: 10 feared dead, houses damaged in flash floods in J&K
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah spoke to Jammu and Kashmir L-G Manoj Sinha and CM Omar Abdullah to take stock of the flood situation in Rajouri district.

title: Gang robs cash and jewels in two houses in Salem
content: On information, Tammampatti police came to the houses and inquired. District Superintendent of Police (SP) S. Kuthalingam also came to the spot and inquired. Special teams were formed to nab the culprits

title: Afghanistan seeks deeper trade ties with India, pitches for easier business access
content: India has also been stepping up engagement with Kabul, with Afghan Agriculture Minister Mawlawi Ataullah Omari visiting New Delhi earlier this month.

title: Opposition walks out of all-party meeting over presence of rebel Trinamool faction
content: The breakaway TMC faction claims to have joined the Nationalist Citizens Party of India; the government is expected to outline its legislative agenda during the meeting

title: Dr. Ambedkar Law University cancels affiliation for three private law colleges
content: The Syndicate of the University, in its meeting early this month, passed a resolution cancelling the affiliation given to these colleges in 2025-26

title: Sonam Wangchuk | Fast and composed
content: The educationist-cum-innovator, who has been on a 21-day fast, has emerged as the face of the CJP’s protest in Delhi demanding the Union Education Minister’s resignation

title: Doctors assault case: Bombay High Court cancels bail of Shiv Sena corporator
content: The court stated that the magistrate had taken the case lightly, given that the accused, accompanied by four others, attacked three doctors in a corporation hospital

title: Health Minister orders inquiry into KMCSL obtaining corporate membership at Thiruvananthapuram Tennis Club
content: The Health Department Principal Secretary was directed to conduct an immediate inquiry based on media reports that ₹19.13 lakh was spent on membership and related expenses from public money

title: 10 feared dead, houses damaged in flash floods in J&K
content: Heavy rainfall in Jammu and Kashmir triggered massive flash floods and resulted in damage to public property in Poonch, Rajouri and Kishtwar districts

title: KPCC cracks whip on party workers feuding over government policy, postings
content: KPCC general secretary Deepthi Mary Varghese has issued a broad set of guidelines, cautioning party apparatchiks against airing broad-brush individualised statements on television talk shows and also social media, which risked feeding the public perception that the party was a divided house

title: What makes Skyroot’s Vikram-1 launch unique? | Explained
content: Skyroot’s Vikram-1 has made India only the third country where a private company has independently placed payloads in orbit. Here’s what makes the mission significant

title: Faith and its fallout
content: In Ayodhya, weeks after allegations of money being siphoned off from donations at the Ram temple surfaced, pilgrims and residents say it has triggered a crisis of trust. Devotees say their faith in Lord Ram and the temple remains intact, but many have grown cautious about donating. Locals report a dip in visitors, though they attribute part of that to seasonal factors as well. As an SIT probe seeks accountability, Ishita Mishra reports from the temple town, where faith, transparency, and the city’s religious tourism hopes hang in the balance

title: Kerala Government declares July 20 an educational holiday on account of the Argentina-Spain FIFA World Cup final
content: Football fans and associations across Kerala greeted the government’s decision with much fanfare. Fans, residents’ associations and sports clubs have set up huge wall-sized LED screens in public squares and parks to broadcast the spirit of the match to the audience.

title: Police action forces end to tribal protest against MP’s Ken-Betwa project after 15 days
content: The police dismantled the protest site, on the banks of Barana river near Kupi village, and symbolic pyres used by the demonstrators

title: Rahul, Kharge write to PM; seek probe into theft of donations at Ayodhya Ram temple
content: Congress leaders demand that the findings and the Trust’s accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been utilised

title: Man found murdered in Kozhikode
content: Involvement of drug addicts suspected based on the statements of some of the local residents in the area

title: Final rites of Chennamma Deve Gowda at foothills of Ranganatha Swamy Betta at Mavinakere
content: The mortal remains of Chennamma will be brought to Holenarasipur by Sunday (July 19, 2026) evening. The public will be allowed to pay their last respects to her at the residence of former Minister H. D. Revanna in Holenarasipur

title: BJP claiming ‘fake credit’ for raising land acquisition compensation for farmers; law made by UPA govt, says Congress
content: The Madhya Pradesh Cabinet had approved an increase in the multiplication factor applicable to land acquisition in rural areas from 1.0 to 2.0. to provide farmers with compensation up to four times the market price

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls July 20 Parliament march India's 'second freedom movement'
content: The note, shared on X through Mr. Wangchuk's account, read: "20th July. India's second freedom movement. Freedom from injustice (like paper leaks). Freedom from fear [my illegal detention]."

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: CM Vijay urged to rename Udhagamandalam
content: The popular name of Ootacamund, or Ooty in short, was inexplicably Sanskritised as Udhagamandalam in 1972

title: With her dance moves, this conservancy worker from Chennai has become the new face of social media algorithm
content: N. Senbagavalli is many things rolled into one: a battery-operated vehicle operator with Urbaser Sumeet in the morning,  an autodriver in the evening, and a successful digital creator on Instagram in between

title: 72nd National Film Awards: Telugu cinema shines with 9 awards; ‘Kalki 2898 AD,’ ‘Pushpa 2’ win big
content: Kalki 2898 AD, Committee Kurrollu, 35-Chinna Katha Kaadu and Pushpa 2: The Rule won two awards each, while Lucky Baskhar bagged one

title: J&K: Amarnath, Vaishno Devi, Shiv Khori pilgrimages suspended amid rain forecast
content: “In view of inclement weather forecast issued by IMD, Shri Amarnathji Yatra shall remain temporarily suspended from both the Pahalgam and Baltal routes with effect from 19.07.2026 as a precautionary measure to ensure the safety and well-being of the pilgrims,” says Kashmir Divisional Commissioner

title: Where football is freedom: A Kolkata mixed-gender team is rewriting rules from margins
content: As the FIFA World Cup commands global attention, children from Kolkata’s Kalighat red-light area are fighting a different battle for the game—one for playgrounds, dignity and the right to dream

title: ‘Wangchuk not alone’: At least 21 continue indefinite hunger strike
content: Three AISA activists enter 21st day of hunger strike; protesters have been advised by doctors to rest as much as possible, conserve energy and speak less

title: Sonam Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised; CJP founder begins fast, vows to march to Parliament on July 20
content: Police cite Delhi High Court order after moving Mr. Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital, where he refused medical interventions; Opposition leaders say government is repressing peaceful dissent

title: News in Frames: The weight of a nation’s food
content: From dawn until dusk, they lift, carry, stack and unload sacks of grain and agricultural commodities, keeping the flow of food moving from farms to markets

title: NMC will no longer issue eligibility certificates to doctors seeking teaching posts in routine cases
content: More complicated cases must take the institutional route, with a fee of ₹25,000 plus tax; move aims to promote institutional accountability in faculty appointments and promotions, reduce admin burden on Board

title: How will U.S. visa policy changes affect students, media professionals, and exchange visitors | Explained
content: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security cites national security risks from Indian and Chinese students and introduces changes in F, J and I categories of visas. PhD scholars, journalists, and exchange visitors will be impacted

title: Delimitation not just an INDIA bloc issue, concerns our Constitutional framework’ says Congress’ K.C. Venugopal
content: “Delimitation is not a Congress issue or an INDIA bloc issue alone; it is a national issue because it concerns India’s democratic and constitutional framework,” Mr. Venugopal said

title: CBSE declares class 10 second board exam results
content: The results for the Class 10 Second Board Exams can be accessed by students through their DigiLocker Results Portal on results.digilocker.gov.in

title: As Vikram-1 reaches orbit, Skyroot faces a steep climb to business success
content: A single launch — crucial though it is — will not catapult Skyroot to success. The global small-satellite launch market is evolving and there are still considerable challenges, including domestic demand and market uncertainties

title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike updates: Wangchuk continues his fast in hospital
content: An independent expert from AIIMS has concurred with the treating team that Sonam Wangchuk needs "immediate medical intervention", hospital said in its latest bulletin

title: Quicksand in Chennai’s Pallikaranai marshland
content: The first expansion of the city of Chennai in modern times happened along its southern stretch. As the government in its wisdom decided to locate the IT corridor in south Chennai, breathless development happened over a couple of decades, right around a rich natural reserve, the Pallikaranai marshland. Today, two decades later, there’s a conflict that pits development against preservation. This report attempts to pull together the several threads that have brought this particular tapestry to the fore today

title: ‘Not just Statehood’: PDP chief lists conditions for joining NC’s Delhi protest
content: In letter to Farooq Abdullah, Mufti says party may skip agitation unless restoration of Article 370, release of political prisoners and lifting of the ban on the Jamaat-e-Islami are included in agenda

title: New tool to help cities quantify greenhouse gas emissions from food waste
content: The Excel-based tool developed by WRI aims to help cities, businesses, and resident associations reduce emissions; it estimates emissions from transportation, treatment, and disposal of waste

title: T.N. government to celebrate State’s history on both July 18 and November 1, says Vijay
content: When the AIADMK was in power, the then Chief Minister Edappadi K. Palaniswami announced that November 1 would be celebrated as Tamil Nadu Day. However, after the DMK came to power in 2021, it changed the observance to July 18

title: Delhi Woman Lay In Pool Of Blood, Killer Husband Kept Changing Cities In UP
content: The 45-year-old woman was found in a pool of blood at her house in Delhi's Seelampur on Thursday evening.

title: 'Why Trinamool Rebels Invited?' Opposition Walks Out Of All-Party Meet
content: The opposition questioned how the NCPI could be part of the all-party meeting, stressing that their merger has not been approved.

title: German Parliament Leader Resigns Due To Pressure Over Surrogate Baby
content: Jens Spahn drew heavy criticism after news emerged that he had become a parent with his husband through a surrogate mother.

title: Sonam Wangchuk's Life Would Be At Risk If Shifted: Sources On Wife's Plea
content: Sonam Wangchuk's wife has said that she has "lost faith" in Safdarjung and sought to shift him to a private facility.

title: Supreme Court To Hear Centre's Plea On Overseas Visa Services Tender Tomorrow
content: Supreme Court agrees to hear Centre's plea against Delhi High Court verdict setting aside Consular, Passport and Visa (CPV) services tender.

title: 11 Dead In Flash Floods In J&K's Rajouri, Several Vehicles Swept Away
content: Flash floods triggered by heavy rains flood Jammu's Rajouri, damaging infrastructure and forcing evacuations.

title: "From Illegal Detention": Sonam Wangchuk's Message Ahead Of Parliament March
content: Sonam Wangchuk's wife has vowed to lead the march when Parliament resumes for the Monsoon Session tomorrow.

title: "Lost Faith In Safdarjung": Sonam Wangchuk's Wife Goes To Delhi High Court
content: Government sources stressed that Sonam Wangchuk's life cannot be put at risk by discharging him from Safdarjung.

title: Trump's Firm To Charge $100,000 A Month For Faster Feed Of His Posts: Report
content: Trump Media & Technology Group, which owns Truth Social, has in recent weeks also pitched a discounted plan of $60,000 per month if the firms sign up for a three-year plan, the sources said

title: Bengaluru Paramedical Student Found Dead In Room. Body Recovered After 3 Days
content: The family said Uday had recently returned from a pilgrimage to Tirupati and had never spoken about any personal issues, including relationship problems.

title: New UK PM Andy Burnham To Drop Digital ID Plan To Focus On Living Costs
content: Resources devoted to the scheme, deemed a "fiasco" by a cross-party committee of lawmakers, will be redirected to Burnham's priorities, a spokesperson said.

title: Why Trump's Saudi Nuclear Deal Is Facing Delay Despite Being Ready
content: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has previously said that if Iran develops a nuclear weapon, Saudi Arabia would also seek one.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Maa Behen, Obsession, And The Gaze That Eats Itself
content: Every society has a woman it has already judged. Every man has a story about her. Cinema has long inherited both.

title: Speeding Porsche Rams Divider On Mumbai's Coastal Road, Driver Escapes Unhurt
content: The driver's life was narrowly saved as the airbags deployed in time, while the car suffered extensive damage in the impact.

title: "Tortured, Forced To Work Day And Night": Hyderabad Woman Stuck In Oman
content: Shabnam Begum said she traveled to Oman on March 26 after a local recruitment agent promised her a job as a housemaid.

title: Ex-Techie With Mental Health Issue Runs To Temple Naked, Then Jumps Into Pond
content: A former IT employee in Medipally was found dead in a pond after running naked amid mental health issues.

title: Nearly 150 turtles washed ashore after a brutal Canadian winter
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds.
